mnemonics: Discrete is Different, Continuous is Continuous
DISCRETE: Discrete Uniform, Independent, Random, Every Time, Ergodic
keyPoints: A discrete random variable can take only a countable number of values.
A continuous random variable can take any value within a given range or interval.
The probability distribution of a discrete random variable is given by P(X=k) = P(X=k) for all k.
The probability density function of a continuous random variable is given by f(x) = f(x) for all x in the range.
The expected value of a discrete random variable is given by E(X) = ΣxP(X=x), where the sum is taken over all possible values of X.
The expected value of a continuous random variable is given by E(X) = ∫xf(x)dx, where the integral is taken over the range of X.
